purpose: Carry forward unanswered owner decisions, implementation questions and zero-gap proof requirements for every applicable framework.
mandatory_questions: What exact outcome and decision must this framework support?
Who owns the framework, each module and final approval?
Which jurisdictions, users, languages and accessibility needs apply?
What is the exact accessible-source denominator and what remains inaccessible?
Which data classes are processed and where may they be stored?
Which tools, APIs, databases, connectors and model providers are allowed or denied?
What free, local, open-source and paid budget ceilings apply?
Which actions require human approval and which are permanently prohibited?
What evidence threshold, source hierarchy and contradiction policy apply?
Which SOPs, OJT packs and competency checks are required?
What tests, red-team cases, acceptance thresholds and independent reviews are required?
What runtime, observability, incident, backup, rollback and retirement controls are required?
What update cadence, alert threshold and continuous-learning retention policy apply?
Which VTI platforms and media rights are approved?
What must remain separated as scientific, engineering, clinical, experimental, symbolic or speculative content?
proof_rule: ZERO_GAP and 100_PERCENT_COVERAGE are prohibited until every dimension has a denominator, processed count, exception list and verifier sign-off.
current_open_loops: Complete raw-source denominator for Health and Spooky2 files.
Implement individual SOP and OJT packs.
Benchmark the Knowledge Fabric retrieval options.
Implement the final visual framework viewer.
Verify staging only after provider configuration and approval.
Pilot GitHub capability scouting with measurable security, usefulness and cost gates.
